steps.title: Spot the key cost signal
steps.content: The passage contrasts radar and sonar (described as “too costly for consumer use”) with a Wi-Fi alternative presented as “a cheap way” to count people and identify gestures. Cost is the defining advantage.

steps.title: Apply to the choices
steps.content: Inexpensive directly captures the “cheap way” descriptor already present in the sentence. The other three—infrequent, unmistakable, and impractical—describe unrelated qualities the passage does not emphasize. That matches choice B — B is correct.
